page: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 9449 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark testing, the F80alds_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Both single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities rank in the top 10% via stress-ng and PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds and large block uncached read/write speeds placing in the top 10%. The instance also achieves top-tier results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its suitability for memory-bound workloads despite the high core count. However, the combination of 80 dedicated high-frequency cores, advanced vector extensions, and substantial local storage makes it highly effective for compute-heavy tasks, compression, static web serving, and database operations. It also shows strong to top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation for small to medium models.
description: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 9449 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The low memory-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, but the high clock speed and dedicated core allocation make it highly efficient for compute-bound workloads. It is well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, data compression, database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 160 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance.

tagline: An 80-core compute-optimized platform with massive local storage, high-frequency AMD processors, and top-tier database and vector processing capabilities.

160.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
9449 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
